info.title: Sim API
info.description: API for executing workflows, querying logs, and managing resources in Sim.

tags.name: Workflows
tags.description: Execute workflows and manage workflow resources

tags.name: Logs
tags.description: Query execution logs and retrieve details

tags.name: Usage
tags.description: Check rate limits and billing usage

tags.name: Audit Logs
tags.description: View audit trail of workspace activity

tags.name: Tables
tags.description: Manage tables and rows for structured data storage

tags.name: Files
tags.description: Upload, download, and manage workspace files

post.description: Execute a deployed workflow. Supports synchronous, asynchronous, and streaming modes. For async execution, the response includes a statusUrl you can poll for results.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the deployed workflow to execute.

requestBody.description: Execution configuration including input values and execution mode options.

input.description: Key-value pairs matching the workflow's defined input fields. Use the Get Workflow endpoint to discover available input fields.

triggerType.description: How this execution was triggered. Defaults to api when called via the REST API. Recorded in execution logs for filtering.

stream.description: When true, returns results as Server-Sent Events (SSE) for real-time block-by-block output streaming.

selectedOutputs.description: List of specific block IDs whose outputs to include in the response. When omitted, all block outputs are returned.

202.description: Asynchronous execution has been queued. Poll the statusUrl for results.

post.description: Cancel a running workflow execution. Only effective for executions that are still in progress.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the workflow.

parameters.name: executionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the execution to cancel.

executionId.description: The ID of the cancelled execution.

get.description: Retrieve all workflows in a workspace with cursor-based pagination.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for your workspace. Found in the Sim dashboard URL or workspace settings.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: Filter results to only include workflows within this folder.

parameters.name: deployedOnly
parameters.description: When true, only return workflows that have been deployed. Useful for listing workflows available for API execution.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of workflows to return per page. Must be between 1 and 100.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned from a previous request's nextCursor field. Omit for the first page.

data.description: Array of workflow summary objects for the current page.

nextCursor.description: Cursor for fetching the next page of results. null when there are no more results.

get.description: Retrieve details for a single workflow, including its input fields and deployment status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique workflow identifier.

200.description: Workflow details including input field definitions.

get.description: Poll the status of an asynchronous workflow execution. Use the jobId returned from the Execute Workflow endpoint when the execution is queued asynchronously.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: The job identifier returned in the async execution response.

200.description: Current status of the job. When completed, includes the execution output.

get.description: List workflow execution logs with advanced filtering and cursor-based pagination. Supports filtering by workflow, trigger type, date range, duration, cost, and more.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for your workspace.

parameters.name: workflowIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of workflow IDs to filter by. Only logs from these workflows will be returned.

parameters.name: folderIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of folder IDs. Returns logs for all workflows within these folders.

parameters.name: triggers
parameters.description: Comma-separated trigger types to filter by: api, webhook, schedule, manual, chat.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Filter logs by severity level. info for successful executions, error for failed ones.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Only return logs after this ISO 8601 timestamp (inclusive).

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Only return logs before this ISO 8601 timestamp (inclusive).

parameters.name: executionId
parameters.description: Filter by an exact execution ID. Useful for looking up a specific run.

parameters.name: minDurationMs
parameters.description: Only return logs where execution took at least this many milliseconds.

parameters.name: maxDurationMs
parameters.description: Only return logs where execution took at most this many milliseconds.

parameters.name: minCost
parameters.description: Only return logs where execution cost at least this amount in USD.

parameters.name: maxCost
parameters.description: Only return logs where execution cost at most this amount in USD.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Filter by the AI model used during execution (e.g., gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4-20250514).

parameters.name: details
parameters.description: Response detail level. basic returns summary fields only. full includes execution data, trace spans, and outputs.

parameters.name: includeTraceSpans
parameters.description: When true, includes block-level execution trace spans with timing and input/output data.

parameters.name: includeFinalOutput
parameters.description: When true, includes the workflow's final output in each log entry.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of log entries to return per page.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned from a previous request's nextCursor field.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort order by execution start time. desc returns newest first.

200.description: A paginated list of execution logs matching the filter criteria.

data.description: Array of log entries for the current page.

nextCursor.description: Cursor for fetching the next page. null when there are no more results.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific log entry, including workflow metadata, execution data, and cost breakdown.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the log entry.

200.description: Detailed log entry with full execution data and cost breakdown.

get.description: Retrieve the full execution state snapshot, including the workflow state at time of execution and detailed metadata.

parameters.name: executionId
parameters.description: The unique execution identifier.

200.description: Full execution state snapshot with workflow state and metadata.

executionId.description: The unique identifier for this execution.

workflowState.description: Snapshot of the workflow configuration at the time of execution.

blocks.description: Map of block IDs to their configuration and state during execution.

edges.description: List of connections between blocks defining the execution flow.

loops.description: Loop configurations defining iterative execution patterns.

executionMetadata.description: Metadata about the execution including timing and cost information.

trigger.description: How the execution was triggered (e.g., api, manual, schedule).

cost.description: Cost breakdown for this execution including per-model details.

get.description: Retrieve audit logs for your organization with cursor-based pagination. Requires an Enterprise subscription and organization admin or owner role.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Filter by action type (e.g., workflow.created, workflow.deployed, member.invited).

parameters.name: resourceType
parameters.description: Filter by resource type (e.g., workflow, workspace, member).

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: Filter by a specific resource ID.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: Filter to audit logs from a specific workspace.

parameters.name: actorId
parameters.description: Filter by the user who performed the action. Must be a member of your organization.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Only return logs after this ISO 8601 timestamp (inclusive).

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Only return logs before this ISO 8601 timestamp (inclusive).

parameters.name: includeDeparted
parameters.description: When true, includes audit logs from users who have left the organization.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of audit log entries to return per page. Must be between 1 and 100.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned from a previous request's nextCursor field.

200.description: A paginated list of audit log entries.

data.description: Array of audit log entries for the current page.

nextCursor.description: Cursor for fetching the next page. null when there are no more results.

get.description: Retrieve a single audit log entry by ID. Requires an Enterprise subscription and organization admin or owner role.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique audit log entry identifier.

get.description: Retrieve your current rate limits, usage spending, and storage consumption for the billing period.

200.description: Current rate limits, usage, and storage information.

get.description: List all tables in a workspace. Returns table metadata including name, schema, and row counts.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The workspace to list tables from.

200.description: List of tables in the workspace.

post.description: Create a new table in a workspace. Define the table schema with typed columns, optional constraints (required, unique), and a name.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL

workspaceId.description: The workspace to create the table in.

name.description: Table name. Must start with a letter or underscore, alphanumeric and underscores only.

get.description: Retrieve a table's metadata, schema, and row count.

parameters.name: tableId
parameters.description: The table ID.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The workspace the table belongs to.

delete.description: Delete a table and all its rows. This action is irreversible.

parameters.name: tableId
parameters.description: The table ID to delete.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The workspace the table belongs to.

get.description: Query rows from a table with optional filtering, sorting, and pagination. Filters and sorts are passed as JSON-encoded query parameters.

parameters.name: tableId
parameters.description: The table to query rows from.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The workspace the table belongs to.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: JSON-encoded filter object. Example: {"status": "active"} or {"age": {"$gt": 18}}.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: JSON-encoded sort object. Example: {"created_at": "desc"}.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum rows to return (1-1000, default 100).

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of rows to skip for pagination (default 0).

rowCount.description: Number of rows returned in this response.

post.description: Insert one or more rows into a table. For a single row, pass a `data` object. For batch insert, pass a `rows` array (up to 1000 rows).

parameters.name: tableId
parameters.description: The table to insert rows into.

data.description: Key-value pairs matching the table schema.

put.description: Bulk update rows matching a filter. All matching rows will have the specified fields updated. Validates against schema and unique constraints.

data.description: Fields to update on matching rows.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to update.

delete.description: Delete rows by filter criteria or by an explicit list of row IDs. Pass either a `filter` object or a `rowIds` array.

requestedCount.description: Number of row IDs requested for deletion (only present when deleting by IDs).

missingRowIds.description: Row IDs that were requested but not found (only present when deleting by IDs).

get.description: Retrieve a single row by its ID.

parameters.name: rowId
parameters.description: The row ID to retrieve.

patch.description: Partially update a single row. Only the provided fields are updated; existing fields are preserved. Data is validated against the table schema.

parameters.name: rowId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the row.

data.description: Fields to update. Only specified fields are changed.

delete.description: Delete a single row by its ID.

parameters.name: rowId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the row.

post.description: Insert a new row or update an existing one based on a unique column value. The table must have at least one column with a unique constraint. If a row with a matching unique value exists, it is updated; otherwise, a new row is inserted. When multiple unique columns exist, specify `conflictTarget` to indicate which column to match on.

data.description: Row data. Must include a value for the conflict target column.

conflictTarget.description: Name of the unique column to match on. Required when the table has multiple unique columns. If the table has exactly one unique column, this can be omitted.

operation.description: Whether the row was inserted or updated.

get.description: List all files in a workspace.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The workspace to list files from.

post.description: Upload a file to a workspace. Send the file as multipart/form-data with a `file` field and a `workspaceId` field. Maximum file size is 100MB. Duplicate filenames within a workspace are not allowed.

workspaceId.description: The workspace to upload the file to.

409.description: A file with the same name already exists in this workspace.

get.description: Download a file's content. Returns the raw file bytes with appropriate Content-Type, Content-Disposition, and Content-Length headers. File metadata is included in custom response headers: X-File-Id, X-File-Name, X-Uploaded-At.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the file.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The workspace the file belongs to.

delete.description: Delete a file from a workspace. This removes both the file content and its metadata. This action is irreversible.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the file to delete.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: The workspace the file belongs to.

apiKey.name: X-API-Key
apiKey.description: Your Sim API key (personal or workspace). Generate one from the Sim dashboard under Settings > API Keys.

ColumnDefinition.description: Definition of a table column including its type and constraints.

name.description: Column name. Must start with a letter or underscore.

required.description: Whether the column requires a value on insert.

unique.description: Whether values in this column must be unique across all rows.

Table.description: A user-defined table with a typed schema.

rowCount.description: Current number of rows in the table.

maxRows.description: Maximum rows allowed by the current billing plan.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the table was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the table was last modified.

TableRow.description: A single row in a table.

data.description: Row data as key-value pairs matching the table schema.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the row was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the row was last modified.

WorkflowSummary.description: Summary representation of a workflow returned in list operations.

description.description: Optional description of what the workflow does.
description.example: Routes incoming support tickets and drafts responses

color.description: Hex color code used for the workflow icon in the dashboard (e.g., #33c482).

folderId.description: The folder this workflow belongs to. null if at the workspace root.

workspaceId.description: The workspace this workflow belongs to.

isDeployed.description: Whether the workflow is currently deployed and available for API execution.

deployedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent deployment. null if never deployed.

runCount.description: Total number of times this workflow has been executed.

lastRunAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent execution. null if never run.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the workflow was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the workflow was last modified.

WorkflowDetail.description: Full workflow representation including input field definitions and configuration.

description.description: Optional description of what the workflow does.
description.example: Routes incoming support tickets and drafts responses

color.description: Hex color code used for the workflow icon in the dashboard.

folderId.description: The folder this workflow belongs to. null if at the workspace root.

workspaceId.description: The workspace this workflow belongs to.

isDeployed.description: Whether the workflow is currently deployed and available for API execution.

deployedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent deployment. null if never deployed.

runCount.description: Total number of times this workflow has been executed.

lastRunAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent execution. null if never run.

variables.description: Workflow-level variables and their current values.

inputs.description: The workflow's input field definitions. Use these to construct the input object when executing the workflow.

fields.description: Map of field names to their type definitions and configuration.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the workflow was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the workflow was last modified.

ExecutionResult.description: Result of a synchronous workflow execution.

success.description: Whether the workflow executed successfully without errors.

executionId.description: Unique identifier for this execution. Use this to query logs or cancel the execution.

output.description: Workflow output keyed by block name and output field. Structure depends on the workflow's block configuration.

error.description: Error message if the execution failed. null on success.

AsyncExecutionResult.description: Response returned when a workflow execution is queued for asynchronous processing.

success.description: Whether the execution was successfully queued.

async.description: Always true for async executions. Use this to distinguish from synchronous responses.

jobId.description: Internal job queue identifier for tracking the execution.

executionId.description: Unique execution identifier. Use this to query execution status or cancel.

statusUrl.description: URL to poll for execution status and results. Returns the full execution result once complete.

LogEntry.description: Summary of a single workflow execution log entry.

executionId.description: Unique execution identifier for this run.

level.description: Log severity. info for successful executions, error for failures.

trigger.description: How the execution was triggered (e.g., api, manual, webhook, schedule, chat).

total.description: Total cost of this execution in USD.

files.description: File outputs produced during execution. null if no files were generated.

LogDetail.description: Detailed log entry with full execution data, workflow metadata, and cost breakdown.

executionId.description: Unique execution identifier for this run.

level.description: Log severity. info for successful executions, error for failures.

trigger.description: How the execution was triggered (e.g., api, manual, webhook, schedule, chat).

workflow.description: Summary metadata about the workflow at the time of execution.

name.description: Workflow name at the time of execution.

description.description: Workflow description at the time of execution.
description.example: Routes incoming support tickets and drafts responses

executionData.description: Detailed execution data including block-level traces and final output.

traceSpans.description: Block-level execution traces with timing, inputs, and outputs for each block that ran.

finalOutput.description: The workflow's final output after all blocks completed.

cost.description: Detailed cost breakdown for this execution.

total.description: Total cost of this execution in USD.

tokens.description: Aggregate token usage across all AI model calls in this execution.

models.description: Per-model cost and token breakdown. Keys are model identifiers (e.g., gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4-20250514).

additionalProperties.description: Cost and token details for a specific model.

input.description: Cost of prompt tokens for this model in USD.

output.description: Cost of completion tokens for this model in USD.

total.description: Total cost for this model in USD.

tokens.description: Token usage for this specific model.

prompt.description: Prompt tokens consumed by this model.

completion.description: Completion tokens generated by this model.

Limits.description: Rate limit and usage information included in every API response.

workflowExecutionRateLimit.description: Current rate limit status for workflow executions.

sync.description: Rate limit bucket for synchronous executions.

async.description: Rate limit bucket for asynchronous executions.

usage.description: Current billing period usage and plan limits.

currentPeriodCost.description: Total spend in the current billing period in USD.

limit.description: Maximum allowed spend for the current billing period in USD.

plan.description: Your current subscription plan (e.g., free, pro, team).

isExceeded.description: Whether the usage limit has been exceeded. Executions may be blocked when true.

RateLimitBucket.description: Rate limit status for a specific execution type.

requestsPerMinute.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per minute.

maxBurst.description: Maximum number of concurrent requests allowed in a burst.

remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current rate limit window.

resetAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the rate limit window resets.

taskId.description: The unique identifier of the job.

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the job started processing.

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the job completed. Present only when status is completed or failed.

duration.description: Duration of the job in milliseconds. Present only when status is completed or failed.

output.description: The workflow execution output. Present only when status is completed.

error.description: Error details. Present only when status is failed.

estimatedDuration.description: Estimated duration in milliseconds. Present only when status is queued or processing.

AuditLogEntry.description: An enterprise audit log entry recording an action taken in the workspace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the audit log entry.

workspaceId.description: The workspace where the action occurred.

actorId.description: The user ID of the person who performed the action.

actorName.description: Display name of the person who performed the action.

actorEmail.description: Email address of the person who performed the action.

action.description: The action that was performed (e.g., workflow.created, member.invited).

resourceType.description: The type of resource affected (e.g., workflow, workspace, member).

resourceId.description: The unique identifier of the affected resource.

resourceName.description: Display name of the affected resource.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the action occurred.

UsageLimits.description: Current rate limits, usage, and storage information for the authenticated user.

rateLimit.description: Rate limit status for workflow executions.

sync.description: Rate limit bucket for synchronous executions.

isLimited.description: Whether the rate limit has been reached.

async.description: Rate limit bucket for asynchronous executions.

isLimited.description: Whether the rate limit has been reached.

authType.description: The authentication type used (api or manual).

currentPeriodCost.description: Total spend in the current billing period in USD.

limit.description: Maximum allowed spend for the current billing period in USD.

plan.description: Your current subscription plan (e.g., free, pro, team).

uploadedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the file was uploaded.

BadRequest.description: Invalid request parameters. Check the details array for specific validation errors.

error.description: Human-readable error message describing the validation failure.

details.description: List of specific validation errors with field-level details.

Unauthorized.description: Invalid or missing API key. Ensure the X-API-Key header is set with a valid key.

Forbidden.description: Access denied. You do not have permission to access this resource. For audit log endpoints, this requires an Enterprise subscription and organization admin/owner role.

NotFound.description: The requested resource was not found. Verify the ID is correct and belongs to your workspace.

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded. Wait for the duration specified in the Retry-After header before retrying.

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

error.description: Human-readable error message with rate limit details.
